---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-22"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(nycflights13)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
```

```{r}
#filtering flights with the important variables we chose
Filtered_flights <- flights[, c("year", "month", "day", "dep_delay", "arr_delay", "dest", "origin", "time_hour")]

Filtered_flights %>% count(year, month, day) %>% filter(n > 1)

summary(Filtered_flights)
#view(Filtered_flights)


#filtering weather with the important variables we chose
filtered_weather <- weather[,c(1,6,8,12,14,15)]

summary(filtered_weather)
#view(filtered_weather)

#combining both filtered datasets by time_hour and origin
cleaned_weather_flights <- inner_join(Filtered_flights, filtered_weather, by = c("origin", "time_hour"))
view(cleaned_weather_flights)
cleaned_weather_flights
cleaned_weather_flights <- drop_na(cleaned_weather_flights)

```





**Q.1** How do holidays/popular vacation times affect delays?

```{r}

# Months with the most departure delays on average
(month_dep_delay <- cleaned_weather_flights |>
  group_by(month) |>
  summarise(avg_dep_delay = mean(dep_delay)) |>
  arrange(desc(avg_dep_delay)))

# Months with the most arrival delays on average
(month_arr_delay <- cleaned_weather_flights |>
  group_by(month) |>
  summarise(avg_arr_delay = mean(arr_delay)) |>
  arrange(desc(avg_arr_delay)))

# Total flights per month
cleaned_weather_flights |>
  group_by(month) |>
  summarise(flight_total = n()) |>
  arrange(desc(flight_total))

# Top 3 months w/ highest delays: July, June, December
# Months with the most amount of flights: August, October, July
# Flights mostly within the 26k - 28.5k range except for February with 23k flights
```

```{r}
library(gridExtra)
bar1 <- ggplot(month_dep_delay, aes(x = month, y = avg_dep_delay, fill = "salmon")) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = month), vjust = 1.5) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

bar2 <- ggplot(month_arr_delay, aes(x = month, y = avg_arr_delay, fill = avg_arr_delay < 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = month), vjust = 1.5) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  ylim(-7, 18)

grid.arrange(bar1, bar2, ncol = 2)
```





**Q.2** Do popular vacation locations mean an increase in flight delays?

```{r}
destinations <- cleaned_weather_flights |>
  group_by(dest) |>
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            avg_arr_delay = mean(arr_delay),
            avg_dep_delay = mean(dep_delay))
  
destinations |> arrange(desc(total_flights))
# lots of flights going into Atlanta (GA), Chicago (IL) and Los Angeles (LAX) in 2013

destinations |> arrange(desc(avg_arr_delay))
# most avg arrival delay: CAE, TUL, OKC

destinations |> arrange(desc(avg_dep_delay))
# most avg dep_delay is: TUL (Oklahoma), CAE (South Carolina), OKC (Oklahoma) 

# ATL - arr: 11.28, dep: 12.41
# ORD - arr: 5.866, dep: 13.44
# LAX - arr: 0.525, dep: 9.35

```


```{r}
percent_delay <- cleaned_weather_flights |>
  group_by(dest) |>
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)

percent_delay |> arrange(desc(percent_arr_delay))
# CAE, OKC, TUL

percent_delay |> arrange(desc(percent_dep_delay))
# JAC, SBN, CAE, TUL, OKC
# Keep in mind JAC and SBN both have less than 25 flights

# CAE: 63% arrival delay and 48% departure delay
# OKC: 50% arrival delay and 43% departure delay
# TUL: 48% arrival delay and 44% departure delay

# Popular airport spots yet they seem to run pretty efficiently
# ATL: 25% arrival delay and 20% departure delay
# ORD: 23% arrival delay and 22% departure delay
# LAX: 20% arrival delay and 17% departure delay
```


**Q.3** Do specific weather conditions and seasons delay flights more than others?
```{r}
#Checking percentage of flights by Seasons
#grouping months into seasons
flights_season <- cleaned_weather_flights %>%
  mutate(season = case_when(
    month %in% c(12, 1, 2) ~ "Winter",
    month %in% c(3, 4, 5) ~ "Spring",
    month %in% c(6, 7, 8) ~ "Summer",
    month %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"
  ))

flights_season

Percent_delay_by_Seasons <- flights_season %>% 
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)

Percent_delay_by_Seasons


#Spring: 24.2% Arrival Delay and 22.4% Departure Delay
#Summer: 28.6% Arrival Delay and 26.6% Departure Delay
#Winter: 26.2% Arrival Delay and 22.2% Departure Delay
#Fall: 15.7% Arrival Delay and 14.7% Departure Delay
```

```{r}
#Checking percentage of flights by rain

#Delays by rain
# groups precipitation by 0.3 inch of rain
flights_rain <- cleaned_weather_flights %>%
  mutate(
    precip_bin = cut(
      precip, 
      breaks = seq(0, max(precip, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.3, by = 0.3),
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      right = FALSE
    )
  )

flights_rain

#finding percentage of delays per 0.3 inches of rain
Avg_delay_per_rain <- flights_rain %>% 
  group_by(precip_bin) %>% 
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)

Avg_delay_per_rain

#0.0 - 0.3 = barely rain: 23.6% Arrival Delay and 21.3% Departure Delay
#0.3 - 0.6 = little rain: 60.5% Arrival Delay and 50.5% Departure Delay
#0.6 - 0.9 = regular rain: 73.9% Arrival Delay and 45.6% Departure Delay
#0.9 - 1.2 = heavy rain: 45% Arrival Delay and 50% Departure Delay
#1.2 above = very heavy rain: 66.6% Arrival Delay and 88.8% Departure Delay

#Note: it seems like although there is a relationship with higher rain and delays, in the heavy rain category it has less delays than the rest

```






**Q.4** Do flight delays occur due to their destination or the origin of the airport?
```{r}

#Checking the Average arrival delay of destinations with top 20 airports
top_dest <- flights %>%
  group_by(dest) %>%
  summarize(avg_arr_delay = mean(arr_delay, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_dep_delay = mean(dep_delay, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  slice_max(avg_arr_delay, n = 20)

ggplot(top_dest, aes(x = reorder(dest, avg_arr_delay), y = avg_arr_delay, fill = dest)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Destination", y = "Average Arrival Delay (min)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(fill = "none")

#compare the departure delay with the destinations with the highest arrival delay
ggplot(top_dest, aes(x = reorder(dest, avg_arr_delay), y = avg_dep_delay, fill = dest)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Destination", y = "Average Departure Delay (min)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(fill = "none")


```

```{r}

#Checking the Average arrival delay of origin
Avg_Origin_delays <- cleaned_weather_flights %>% 
  group_by(origin) %>% 
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)
  
  
Avg_Origin_delays
#The 3 airports in New York seems to run efficiently
#EWR: 25.5% Arrival Delay and 24.5% Departure Delay
#JFK: 22.9% Arrival Delay and 20% Departure Delay
#LGA: 22.3% Arrival Delay and 18.8% Departure Delay


#compared to the nation average
#using data from destinations to find national average
Nation_Average_delays <- percent_delay %>% 
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
             mean_avg_arr = mean(percent_arr_delay),
      mean_avg_dep = mean(percent_dep_delay))
    
 Nation_Average_delays   

#National average: 26.1% Arrival Delay and 25% Departure Delay
 
```
